Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares closed nearly 2% higher on Thursday, as chipmakers jumped following upbeat demand forecasts from Nvidia that eased investor worries around artificial intelligence stocks.

** The benchmark KOSPI closed up 75.34 points, or 1.92%, at 4,004.85, after two consecutive sessions of losses.

** Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on Wednesday shrugged off concerns about an AI bubble as the company surprised Wall Street with accelerating growth after several quarters of slowing sales.

** "Today's gains in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix show the AI rally is yet to come to an end," said Huh Jae-hwan, an analyst at Eugene Investment Securities.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 4.25% and peer SK Hynix gained 1.60%, leading the benchmark index higher.

** Battery maker LG Energy Solution climbed 0.80%, but Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 0.76% and 0.96%, respectively.

** Of the total 928 traded issues, 751 shares advanced, while 141 declined.

** Foreign investors were net buyers of shares worth 641.3 billion won ($436.79 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,467.9 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.04% higher than its previous close at 1,468.5.

** In money and debt markets, December futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) slipped by 0.10 point to 105.81.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 2.8 basis points to 2.903%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 3.5 basis points to 3.320%.

($1 = 1,468.2100 won)

data.title: Italy eyes first rating upgrade from Moody's in 23 years
data.description:
Key points:
  • Moody's rates Italy Baa3, with positive outlook
  • Italy's 2025 budget deficit seen respecting EU ceiling
  • Italy has had string of ratings boosts from other agencies
  • Moody's has not raised Italy's rating since May 2002

By Alessia Pe

Moody's review of Italy on Friday may yield the first rating upgrade for almost a quarter of a century, analysts say, reflecting growing confidence in the public finances of the euro zone's third largest economy.

The agency lifted Italy's outlook in May this year to positive from stable while keeping the rating at 'Baa3', the lowest rung of investment grade, citing a stronger-than-expected fiscal performance and a steadier political backdrop.

Since then Giorgia Meloni's government has cut its 2025 budget deficit goal to 3% of gross domestic product, respecting the European Union's limit a year ahead of schedule helped by stronger tax revenues and lower debt servicing costs.

RATING UNCHANGED SINCE 2018

"Italy's fiscal outturns keep surprising positively, supported by the unwinding of a massive fiscal stimulus, job-rich and tax-rich GDP growth and EU-financed fiscal support," Citi analysts wrote in a weekly report.

They added that "positive fundamentals have not yet been captured in the rating, which remains close to 2015-16 levels."

Moody's has not improved Italy's rating since May 2002, when it passed from Aa3 to Aa2, and the rating has not changed since a downgrade in October 2018.

Positive action from the agency would round off a string of ratings boosts for Italy in the autumn round of reviews, with Fitch upgrading it to BBB+, DBRS lifting it to A (low), and Scope improving the outlook on its BBB+ to positive from stable.

S&P Global was the only agency to make no change, confirming the sovereign at 'BBB+' with a stable outlook, having hiked the rating just six months earlier.

Italian government bonds have rallied strongly: the gap between 10-year BTPs over safe-haven German Bunds - a key gauge of the extra return investors demand to hold Italian debt – has narrowed by around about 40 basis points since early September to around 75 basis points, near a 15-year low.

Analysts at UniCredit said a potential upgrade on Friday would mark "another step in the constructive trend that has characterised the overall assessment of Italy's creditworthiness," noting that among major rating agencies Moody's remains the most cautious.

BBVA assigned a 60% probability to an upgrade, arguing that Moody's decision not to downgrade France but to merely move its outlook to negative in October "might be interpreted as a signal supporting a positive action on Italy."

STAGNANT ECONOMY

Still, challenges remain. Italy's aging population and high debt burden weigh on long-term sustainability, while global trade tensions add uncertainty to its already chronically weak economic growth.

Gross domestic product stagnated in the third quarter after shrinking by 0.1% in the second, and the government last month cut its forecast for full-year growth to just 0.5%, after 0.7% expansion last year.

Yet for now, ratings agencies and markets are focused on the positives and rewarding Rome for its fiscal discipline and political stability, with Meloni's government still popular after three years in office.

data.title: Klondaika Champions Community and Culture with Major New Sponsorships in Latvian Entertainment
data.description:
Strategic partnerships with popular quiz series "Liela viktorina" and new lifestyle fishing show "Lidakas un legendas Kurzeme" signal a deep investment in diverse local leisure activities.

Riga, Latvia--(Newsfile Corp. - November 20, 2025) - Klondaika, a cornerstone of Latvia's entertainment and leisure landscape, is proud to announce two significant new strategic sponsorships, further solidifying its commitment to fostering community spirit and supporting diverse cultural pastimes. The company is teaming up as a key sponsor for the widely popular "Lielā viktorīna" (The Big Quiz) series of engaging events. In a distinct second partnership, Klondaika is also sponsoring a team of fishing experts, an agreement which includes brand integration into a new adventure television show titled "Līdakas un leģendas. KURZEM" (Pikes and Legends).

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8530/274712_98b947fce4b3485f_001full.jpg

These initiatives represent a thoughtfully expanded sponsorship portfolio, moving beyond traditional entertainment to connect with Latvians through their shared passions, hobbies, and interests.

A New Hub for Intellectual Entertainment

The first partnership sees Klondaika joining forces with "Lielā viktorīna," a beloved entertainment format that challenges participants in both online and offline quiz events. This collaboration is designed to create a new home for intellectual and social fun, bringing people together for an evening of friendly, witty competition.

The quiz series, which playfully invites "everyone who is smarter than me" ("Pasākums visiem, kuri ir gudrāki par mani"), aligns perfectly with Klondaika's mission to provide dynamic and social leisure experiences. The sponsorship was recently celebrated with a highly successful live event at the Klondaika Purvciems venue, which saw teams gather for a spirited challenge. This partnership transforms Klondaika's venues into vibrant community hubs, where guests can enjoy a high-quality social atmosphere. This move is part of a broader strategy, as Klondaika offers a wide range of entertainment options designed to cater to a multitude of tastes, from high-energy gaming to relaxed, social gatherings.

Celebrating Latvian Lifestyle and Nature

In a distinct move that highlights the brand's versatility, Klondaika has also embarked on a major sponsorship of a new television show dedicated to fishing. This brand integration partnership with the "Līdakas un leģendas. KURZEM" adventure series connects Klondaika with one of Latvia's most passionate and dedicated communities: anglers and outdoor enthusiasts.

This sponsorship goes beyond simple advertising, weaving the Klondaika brand into the narrative of the show itself, which explores the rich fishing culture and natural beauty of the Kurzeme region. The collaboration, already featured in publications like Copeslietas.lv, demonstrates Klondaika's authentic interest in supporting niche hobbies and celebrating the Latvian lifestyle. It's a strategic decision to engage with audiences in the spaces where their passions lie, whether that's in a vibrant café or on the serene banks of a river.

A Dual Strategy for Deeper Engagement

Together, these two sponsorships illustrate Klondaika's dynamic and multifaceted community engagement strategy. On one hand, the brand is fostering urban, social, and intellectual entertainment through its "Lielā viktorīna" events. On the other, it is celebrating the national pastime of fishing, synonymous with nature, patience, and adventure.

This dual approach underscores a deep understanding of its audience, recognizing that modern entertainment consumers seek a variety of experiences. By investing in both cerebral challenges and rugged lifestyle programming, Klondaika is building connections that are both broad and deep. These initiatives are more than just sponsorships; they are a direct investment in the cultural and social fabric of Latvia, creating more opportunities for people to connect, compete, and share their passions.

As these partnerships develop, audiences can expect more exciting live quiz events and engaging content from the fishing series. To stay informed on all upcoming events and brand news, visit the official Klondaika website.

About Klondaika

Klondaika is a leading and long-standing provider of entertainment and leisure in Latvia. Renowned for its commitment to quality, innovation, and responsible entertainment, Klondaika operates a wide network of venues that offer state-of-the-art gaming, comfortable lounges, and premium hospitality, including the popular "Klondaika cafe" brand. With a firm belief in community engagement, Klondaika continuously seeks to enrich the Latvian cultural landscape by supporting a diverse arrayof local events, sports, and lifestyle projects.

Paula Svara-Lallo

SIA Klondaika

Email support@klondaika.lv

Website https://klondaika.lv/
